items.title: US stock markets: Dow Jones hits record high, Nasdaq sinks 4.6% - What shook Wall Street this week?
items.summary: For the week ended June 26, the Dow Jones Industrial Average outperformed its peers, gaining about 0.6% to close at a record high. The broader S&amp;P 500, however, declined 2.0%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite tumbled 4.6%.

items.title: F&O Talk: Nifty to remain rangebound, says Sudeep Shah; picks 8 stocks for next week
items.summary: Indian stock markets concluded the week with modest gains, as Sensex and Nifty erased earlier surges on monthly expiry day. Analysts observe Nifty's consolidation, with key support at 23,750-23,800 and resistance at 24,250-24,300. Bank Nifty shows outperformance, eyeing 58,800 for further upside. Derivatives data suggests improving sentiment, with FIIs reducing bearish bets. Sectors like Private Banks, Auto, and Pharma appear strong.

items.title: AI rout exposes Wall Street's $270 billion speculation machine
items.summary: A recent tech sell-off exposed how modern speculation amplifies both rallies and reversals. Retail investors' enthusiasm for leveraged products tied to AI chips and SpaceX, alongside financial engineering in crypto, led to rapid gains and equally swift losses. These products, designed for faster, more concentrated bets, are increasingly shaping market dynamics, bringing a casino-like element to trading and posing significant risks for unsophisticated investors.

items.title: ETMarkets Smart Talk | Corporate bonds are emerging as a compelling alternative to fixed deposits: Nikhil Aggarwal
items.summary: As fixed deposit rates decline, Indian investors are increasingly exploring corporate bonds for better post-tax returns. This shift, driven by improving market access and regulatory reforms, is causing retail participation in bonds to surge. Experts highlight that while higher yields signal greater risk, careful evaluation of credit ratings, issuer health, and liquidity is crucial for informed investment decisions in this evolving fixed-income landscape.

items.title: U.S. Judge won't immediately dismiss criminal charges against Gautam Adani
items.summary: Adani was charged ​in ‌2024 with agreeing to bribe Indian government officials so a subsidiary of ‌his Adani Group ​could win approval to develop a solar ⁠plant, and then misleading U.S. investors

items.title: Two-year yields dip as Hormuz flows rise, hopes on inflation grow
items.summary: Treasury yields dipped Friday as falling oil prices, linked to increased Strait of Hormuz shipments, sparked hopes of easing inflation. While the Federal Reserve remains watchful of price pressures, traders are assessing the likelihood of future rate hikes. Despite some hawkish signals from policymakers, the prevailing sentiment suggests a pause in rate increases, with economists largely expecting rates to hold steady this year.

items.title: European shares slip as tech selloff bites, Zalando slides
items.summary: European markets retreated from record highs Friday, with tech stocks mirroring global weakness. Zalando shares plunged after a German probe into its accounts, impacting the retail sector. Investors grappled with AI's uncertain impact on tech, while chipmakers saw declines. Despite the dip, the STOXX 600 managed modest weekly gains, buoyed by easing oil concerns and Europe's lighter tech exposure.

items.title: Stocks to buy below  ₹100: Mehul Kothari of Anand Rathi recommends three shares to buy or sell
items.summary: Next week, Bank Nifty's trading range is set between 57,000 and 59,000, with breakouts suggesting future trends. Analyst Mehul Kothari advises buying Trident, UCO Bank, and MMTC under  ₹100, as the Indian market remains strong amid mixed signals and easing crude oil prices.

items.title: Wall Street Week Ahead: Jobs data, rate bets in focus as US stocks close solid first half
items.summary: Upcoming jobs data could signal a strong U.S. economy, potentially leading to interest rate hikes and stock market jitters, especially for tech shares. While major indexes are set for a solid first half, recent volatility in semiconductor stocks highlights investor concerns about AI-driven profits and overheated markets. Inflation remains a key focus for the Federal Reserve, with a hawkish stance suggesting rate hikes are back on the table.

items.title: Dollar declines for second straight day but set for weekly gain
items.summary: The dollar dipped for a second day as easing inflation and falling oil prices tempered expectations for Federal Reserve rate hikes. Despite this pullback, the greenback remains strong for the week and month. Meanwhile, the yen hovers near a critical level, raising intervention concerns. Analysts suggest a cautious approach to the dollar against the yen ahead of key U.S. jobs data.

items.title: Oil prices dive as more tankers move through Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Crude oil prices experienced a significant drop, heading for steep weekly losses as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz increased, easing supply worries. This comes after a cargo vessel was struck near Oman, with reports suggesting Iranian involvement. Despite the incident, oil continues to flow, and analysts anticipate an oversupply, further pressuring prices. Saudi Aramco has also resumed loading operations.

items.title: Gold gains as dollar weakens; still on track for fourth straight weekly loss
items.summary: Gold prices saw a modest uptick on Friday, buoyed by a weaker dollar and slightly eased expectations of U.S. interest rate hikes following inflation data. Despite this rebound, gold is still on track for its fourth consecutive weekly dip.  This slight recovery comes after hitting a seven-month low earlier in the week, with analysts pointing to the inverse relationship between gold and rising interest rates.

items.title: IIT Bombay, New York University announce collaboration in engineering education, research
items.summary: IIT Bombay has teamed up with the State University of New York Old Westbury to enhance education and research in science and engineering. This partnership will delve into cutting-edge areas like artificial intelligence, aiming to establish collaborative programs and research projects.

items.title: Buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends three stocks to buy on Monday - 29 June 2026
items.summary: Sumeet Bagadia recommended these three buy-or-sell stocks: Dr Reddy's Labs, M&amp;M, and Tata Consumer.

items.title: Why post-harvest loss is a farmer income story — not just a food security one
items.summary: What a farmer actually earns is determined not by what he grows, but by how much of it survives the journey from field to market

items.title: The next decade of agriculture will be defined by resource efficiency, not land ownership
items.summary: The next green revolution is about intelligence; it’s about producing smarter, not just more

items.title: The invisible layer powering agriculture: Why software will matter more than soil
items.summary: The software layer above the soil will matter more- it is time the invisible layer became the most invested-in one

items.title: Trump administration clears Anthropic to roll out Claude Mythos 5 to over 100 organisations: Report
items.summary: The US government has permitted Anthropic to release its Claude Mythos 5 AI model to trusted partners.&nbsp;

items.title: Indian IT stays ashore as Accenture surfs the cybersecurity wave
items.summary: High costs and talent shortages leave domestic firms leaning on slower organic growth while global rivals deploy massive capital to rapidly scale security platforms.

items.title: Why Equirus is avoiding loss-making startups in its  ₹1,500 crore fund
items.summary: Equirus new fund launch comes as India’s startup funding market becomes more selective, even though early-stage capital has held up better than late-stage funding.

items.title: The twin devils that continue to plague AI
items.summary: There are architectural flaws in chatbot interactions that refuse to go away and make it difficult to trust them.

items.title: Top 10 Flexicap Mutual Funds in 5 years: Rs 32 lakh lump sum investment in No. 1 scheme has turned into Rs 72.4 lakh
items.summary: Flexicap funds are considered suitable for wealth creation, children's education, retirement planning, and buying a house. However, returns are not guaranteed in flexicap funds, as they are linked to the market.

items.title: Consumer companies ramp up festive production on demand hope
items.summary: Leading electronics contract manufacturers such as Dixon Technologies and PG Electroplast highlighted a "high" double-digit increase in orders across categories including televisions, lighting products, washing machines, and refrigerators. PG Electroplast, which supplies to brands such as LG, Midea, Voltas, Whirlpool, and Daikin, said its festive order book has grown by 40% over last year. Dixon counts Samsung, Xiaomi, Haier, and Philips among its customers.

items.title: US stocks: US market ends lower as tech stocks weigh on sentiment
items.summary: Wall Street ended lower on Friday, with AI chip stocks experiencing a significant downturn while Moderna and other healthcare companies surged. Investors are weighing the long-term potential of AI against concerns about the substantial investment required for data centers.  Inflationary pressures, fueled by energy prices and chip costs, remain a concern, impacting companies like Apple and prompting discussions about potential interest rate hikes.

items.title: ET Graphics: From budget to premium, gadget Prices Rise
items.summary: Indian consumers are facing a significant price hike on smartphones, laptops, and tablets, impacting both budget and premium devices. This surge is primarily due to a global shortage of essential memory chips, as manufacturers divert production towards lucrative AI data center components. A weakening rupee further exacerbates this inflationary trend, making electronics increasingly expensive for the masses.

items.title: After 5-year hot run, luxe car sales may slow down
items.summary: India's luxury car market is experiencing its first slowdown in over five years, with sales stalling in early 2026. Heightened global uncertainties, volatile markets, and a weaker rupee are making affluent buyers cautious. Luxury carmakers have also increased prices, further impacting demand. This contrasts sharply with the booming mass-market segment, highlighting a shift in buyer sentiment among the wealthy.

items.title: Cold war over ice cream: A three-decade old Vadilal family feud returns to Bombay High Court
items.summary: A three-decade-old family pact dividing the Vadilal ice cream brand is now a legal battleground. The Mumbai branch has sued the Ahmedabad branch in Bombay High Court, seeking to prevent interference with its ice cream and juice business. Allegations of sabotage and failed buyout attempts fuel the dispute, with a court order pending on interim relief.

items.title: Apple's latest price hike hits India hardest as MacBooks, iPads get up to 70% costlier
items.summary: Apple has significantly increased prices for MacBooks, iPads, and other devices in India, with hikes ranging from 14-70%. This sharp rise is attributed to a weaker rupee, high import duties, and local taxes. Analysts suggest this could lead to increased grey market imports due to price differences compared to the US and EU.

items.title: Smartphone brands brace for 9-10% festive sales drop as higher prices dampen demand
items.summary: Smartphone sales are projected to dip by 9-10% this festive season as rising handset prices dampen consumer demand. Brands are strategizing with discounts, but these will be on inflated baseline prices. Manufacturers are securing components early to manage costs, while marketing budgets are being trimmed. Expect a focus on financing options like no-cost EMIs to encourage purchases.

items.title: India initiates anti-dumping investigation into steel imports from China, Japan, Russia
items.summary: India has launched an anti-dumping investigation into hot rolled steel imports from China, Japan, and Russia. JSW Steel and Jindal Steel allege these products are being sold at unfairly low prices, harming domestic manufacturers. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies found initial evidence of dumping, with significant price differences. This probe, covering imports from 2022-2025, aims to assess the impact on India's steel industry.

items.title: India initiates anti-dumping investigation into steel imports from China, Japan, Russia
items.summary: India has launched an anti-dumping investigation into hot rolled steel imports from China, Japan, and Russia. JSW Steel and Jindal Steel allege these products are being sold at unfairly low prices, harming domestic manufacturers. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies found initial evidence of dumping, with significant price differences. This probe, covering imports from 2022-2025, aims to assess the impact on India's steel industry.

items.title: CBIC rolls out green channel to speed emergency imports during oil spill crises
items.summary: In a significant move to bolster environmental protection, the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs has launched a dedicated 'green channel' for swift customs clearance during oil and hazardous spill emergencies. This initiative, driven by a Coast Guard request, aims to expedite the movement of crucial response equipment, thereby enhancing India's preparedness for marine pollution incidents and fulfilling international maritime commitments.

items.title: El Nino may force India to boost rice-based ethanol output for E20 goal
items.summary: India might need to boost rice-based ethanol production for E20 fuel blending by 2026-27. El Nino's impact on sugarcane and maize crops could reduce their availability for ethanol. With tight sugar supplies expected, the government may prioritize domestic demand, limiting sugar diversion. This scenario could necessitate a significant increase in rice used for ethanol to meet blending targets.

items.title: Punjab drug body finds critical lapses at Jackson Laboratories' manufacturing facility in Amritsar
items.summary: The Punjab FDA has taken decisive action by revoking the manufacturing licence of Jackson Laboratories in Amritsar due to severe regulatory infractions. This drastic step comes after investigations tied the company’s oxytocin injections to the tragic deaths of five mothers in Rajasthan. Findings show significant non-compliance with essential manufacturing regulations, presenting grave concerns over hygiene and product testing.

items.title: Goldman Sachs raises India FY27 growth forecast to 6.5%
items.summary: Goldman Sachs has boosted India's FY27 growth outlook to 6.5%, citing lower oil prices and easing supply issues. The investment bank also anticipates a dip in inflation to 4.9%. Despite initial consumption resilience during Middle East tensions, a slight softening in investment was noted. Goldman Sachs projects a 50 basis point repo rate hike by the RBI in 2026, while acknowledging weather-related risks to rural demand.

items.title: Shelf-life norms for imported drugs may be relaxed to reduce wastage
items.summary: India is set to revise rules for imported medicines, proposing a 12-month minimum residual shelf life instead of the current 60% rule. This aims to ease business and reduce wastage, particularly for drugs with longer expiry dates. However, biological and radiopharmaceutical products will retain the stricter 60% requirement due to public health concerns. Other quality and safety regulations remain unchanged.

items.title: HDFC Bank legal review finds no basis for former part-time chairman’s resignation claims
items.summary: Three external law firms reviewed board records, meeting minutes, communications and interviews, concluding there was no evidence supporting Chakraborty’s resignation concerns or claims

items.title: Centre plans Rs 12,000 crore incentive scheme to spur private electric bus adoption
items.summary: The government is planning a significant incentive package, potentially worth ₹12,000 crore, to boost electric bus adoption by private fleet operators. This scheme could include interest subvention, credit guarantees, and capital subsidies, aiming to encourage the deployment of 50,000 electric buses over a decade. Officials anticipate this will unlock substantial private investment, mirroring successful government initiatives for public transport.

items.title: India and Switzerland step up innovation partnership with focus on startups, research
items.summary: India and Switzerland are boosting science and technology ties, combining India's scale with Swiss innovation for citizen solutions and startups. Swissnex is a key facilitator, connecting innovators and supporting market entry. Switzerland is keen on India's digital infrastructure, hinting at joint ventures. New initiatives are expected soon, building on existing research programs in geoscience, healthcare, and drug development, alongside startup exchange programs.

items.title: Law firms hired by HDFC Bank find no basis for governance doubts raised by former chair Chakraborty
items.summary: With the legal review out of the way, HDFC bank’s board is likely to take a call on chief executive Jagdishan’s reappointment

items.title: Centre invites bids for green urea plants to curb import reliance
items.summary: The department on Friday held a pre-EoI meeting with public and private sector companies, including NTPC, the Solar Energy Corporation of India (SECI), fertiliser manufacturers, technology providers and green hydrogen developers, to discuss the proposed project framework.

items.title: 25% of Indian tech services firms have moved AI experiments into production level: Nasscom
items.summary: ‘Indian IT is currently generating up to $12 billion in AI services revenue, with more than 2 million professionals skilled in AI and 100,000 to 200,000 trained in advanced AI capabilities’

items.title: Cabinet Secretariat cracks down on red tape, orders faster clearances for Centre's key projects
items.summary: The Cabinet Secretariat is pushing for faster government processes and reduced red tape to expedite key central projects. New directives aim to streamline inter-ministerial consultations and ensure timely Cabinet approvals.  A cascaded review mechanism before PRAGATI meetings and enhanced monitoring of the e-Samiksha portal are being implemented to boost efficiency and outcomes across ministries and states.

items.title: IMO pauses its Gulf vessel evacuation plan after Iran attacks ship
items.summary: The attack followed a joint declaration by the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) states, including Oman, after a meeting attended by the United States, stating that there should be no “tolls, fees, or attempts to assert control over the strait.”

items.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold extends rebound above $4,100; silver gains for second day as dollar weakens
items.summary: Gold and silver prices increased for the second day, aided by a weaker US dollar. Gold rose to $4,111, despite continuing a decline, while silver reached $59.53. Both metals are on track for significant weekly losses as gold's June decline reaches 11%.

items.title: Chhattisgarh's Textile Park bags Rs 445 crore investment; over 11,000 jobs expected
items.summary: Under the leadership of Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai, the state is looking to establish a new industrial identity beyond mining and traditional manufacturing.

items.title: Seafarers feel forgotten, shipping should not be used as collateral: IMO chief amid US-Iran conflict
items.summary: Shipping must be kept out of geopolitical conflicts, urged the International Maritime Organization chief, as seafarers "feel forgotten."  He highlighted the tragic loss of Indian lives in recent attacks, emphasizing the need for better seafarer protection. The IMO is working to support those still in dangerous regions, ensuring their safety and connection with families amidst escalating tensions.

items.title: Piyush Goyal holds strategic talks with Rolls-Royce delegation to bolster advanced manufacturing in India
items.summary: Union Minister Piyush Goyal met with a Rolls-Royce delegation to explore strengthening industrial and technological ties, highlighting the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) as a key opportunity. Goyal emphasized CETA's potential to deepen investments, accelerate tech partnerships, and build resilient supply chains, benefiting both nations. He also reassured that the pact is designed to boost economic growth and job creation without harming domestic sectors.

items.title: SEBI rejects Reliance Infra, Anil Ambani plea to settle ₹6,526-cr case
items.summary: In show-cause notice, regulator seeks restoration of funds pending recovery along with applicable interest

items.title: IMF chief economist says Fed reduction in rate guidance is 'entirely appropriate'
items.summary: The IMF's chief economist, Pierre-Olivier Gourinchas, has endorsed Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's move to reduce explicit forward guidance on monetary policy. Gourinchas stated that overly rigid guidance proved costly when inflation surged, as it tied the Fed's hands. While acknowledging the need for some long-term market guidance, he believes explicit, strong commitments are no longer tenable, suggesting a more flexible approach is appropriate for central banks.

items.title: Crude oil prices tumble 5% as Strait of Hormuz traffic rebounds; Brent plunges 20% in June
items.summary: Brent crude prices plummeted to $72 as supply concerns eased with resumed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The weekly decline reaches 10%, while June losses hit 20%, reflecting the impact of improved stability in Middle Eastern oil exports.

items.title: Small businesses, big impact
At Sid’s Farm, the pursuit of milk begins with uncompromising standards
items.summary: Sid's Farm is strengthening consumer trust through rigorous daily quality checks and a parent-first approach to dairy production. The company invests over ₹15 lakh every month in antibiotic residue testing while rewarding farmers for maintaining quality standards. Its #HoldUsToIt campaign encourages consumers to question and verify its practices, promoting transparency over traditional brand promises. By aligning supply-chain integrity with food safety, Sid’s Farm aims to set a new benchmark for trust in India's dairy sector.

items.title: Investment banks, rating agencies lower gold outlook after dropping over 25% since Jan 29
items.summary: Near-term for the precious metal has become challenging, driven by US Fed policy signals

items.title: Rajesh Exports says ED search found no inventory or cash discrepancies; no valuables seized
items.summary: The company told exchanges that the ED search concluded without any inventory or cash discrepancies and that no valuables were seized, even as the FEMA investigation continues.

items.title: Kolkata warehouse collapse death toll rises to 15, 6 arrested
items.summary: The incident occurred around noon on Wednesday when the roof of the under-construction warehouse at Taratala suddenly collapsed

items.summary: The series will discuss MSMEs’ contribution to the Indian growth story.

items.title: Switching to Nothing Phone (4a) Pro? 10 pros and cons to keep in mind
items.summary: Nothing Phone (4a) Pro Review: The Nothing Phone (4a) Pro falls into the premium mid-range smartphone category in India. Depending on the RAM and storage configuration, the starting price of this phone ranges from Rs 44,450 to Rs 55,999. But is it really that good in practice? Is it worth buying? Let's find out in this review.

items.title: BMW launches X6 M60i xDrive in India at Rs 1.77 crore; packs 530 hp V8 engine
items.summary: BMW X6 M60i xDrive Launched in India: BMW India has launched the new BMW X6 M60i xDrive in the country at an ex-showroom price of Rs 1.77 crore. The performance Sports Activity Coupé (SAC) will be available as a Completely Built-Up Unit (CBU).

items.title: Silver Consumer Electricals concludes Rs 150 cr pre-IPO secondary share sale
items.summary: Silver Consumer Electricals, renowned for its pumps and motors, has successfully raised Rs 150 crore through a pre-IPO secondary share sale. A 2.59% stake was sold by a promoter group member to investor Riyaz Suterwala, paving the way for its upcoming initial public offering. With its DRHP filed last August, the Rajkot-based company aims to secure up to Rs 1,400 crore to address debt and enhance corporate operations.

items.title: Fidelity Investments buys 2 pc stake in Lodha Developers for Rs 1,864 crore
items.summary: Fidelity Investments has strategically acquired a 2% stake in Lodha Developers, amounting to Rs 1,864 crore, through open market operations. The acquisition involves nearly 2 crore shares purchased from promoter entities Hightown Constructions and Homecraft Developers, resulting in a reduction of the promoter group's shareholding to 70.28%. Notably, Lodha Developers has recently reported a remarkable 9% increase in net profit for the March quarter, attributed to heightened income levels.

items.title: Uber India, South Asia, President Prabhjeet Singh steps down after 11 years
items.summary: Sources said Uber's India leadership team will continue to manage day-to-day operations, closely supported by the Asia Pacific (APAC) leadership, until a transition is finalized.

items.title: Air India may restore curtailed international services as West Asia crisis eases: CEO
items.summary: Air India had rationalised services on select international routes between June and August 2026 owing to continued airspace restrictions over certain regions and record-high jet fuel prices

items.title: Hormuz traffic eases, ships continue using disputed Oman route
items.summary: Despite a projectile striking a vessel, ships are continuing to use a non-Iranian-approved passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Traffic levels saw a slight dip from Wednesday's peak, but many commodity vessels, including oil tankers, are still transiting. Iran insists only its designated routes are authorized, leading to a temporary pause in a mariner evacuation program.

items.title: Air India may restore some flight schedules amid easing West Asia conflict: Sources
items.summary: According to airline sources, Air India CEO Campbell Wilson communicated to employees that the easing of the conflict has created a more stable operating environment, which in turn has increased available airspace and led to a significant moderation in fuel prices.

items.title: Foxconn infuses Rs 351 crore into India arm
items.summary: In a substantial development, Taiwanese electronics powerhouse Foxconn has committed an impressive USD 37.2 million (around Rs 351 crore) to its Indian subsidiary, Foxconn Hon Hai Technology India Mega Development Private Limited.

items.title: Disruptions cannot be reversed overnight: ASSOCHAM chief
items.summary: India must combine its labour advantage with automation while pursuing calibrated trade liberalisation and investment reforms, says Nirmal K Minda

items.title: 'Seize the moment': Piyush Goyal sees India-UK trade pact unlocking 'floodgates' for business
items.summary: India and the UK are buzzing with optimism as their Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) takes effect on July 15. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal, leading a large business delegation, expressed confidence that the pact will significantly boost bilateral trade and strengthen the partnership. He emphasized that "this is the time, the right time" for enhanced collaboration, drawing parallels to Prime Minister Modi's sentiment.

items.title: Is Air India planning to reverse recent international flight reductions? Here's what CEO Campbell Wilson said in a letter to staff
items.summary: Air India Latest Update: Air India may reverse some of the schedule reductions introduced in recent months if geopolitical conditions remain stable, CEO Campbell Wilson said in a letter to employees.

items.title: Fertiliser Ministry prepares green urea roadmap, plans a pilot plant in Andhra Pradesh
items.summary: ICAR has been conducting trials at its different locations since the last Rabi season on the effectiveness of green ammonia on some select crops like paddy, oilseeds and sugarcane

items.title: India well-poised for rapid growth despite risks from West Asia war and El Nino: ITC
items.summary: Despite facing geopolitical instability and weather-related challenges, India's economy is expected to grow robustly at 6.9% in FY27. This growth is bolstered by favorable agricultural conditions, significant government infrastructure investments, and a resurgence in both rural and urban demand. While global tensions present risks, India's demographic advantages and ongoing digital transformation solidify its foundation for long-term expansion and competitiveness on the global stage.

items.title: Can an OpenAI IPO delay derail the AI chip rally? Here's why it matters
items.summary: A potential delay in OpenAI's IPO has triggered selling in global technology stocks, impacting major players like SoftBank. As concerns over AI investment returns grow, investors are increasingly selective in their stock choices, marking a shift in sentiment within the technology sector.

items.title: ITC  to implement water security programmes in Bengaluru, Chennai
items.summary: These initiatives are aimed at strengthening urban water resilience by addressing groundwater depletion and reducing flood risks during extreme rainfall, the conglomerate said in its annual report

items.title: Fragmented EV platforms, lack of component standardisation impacting economies of scale
items.summary: IEEFA and JMK Research & Analytics report urges standardisation and stronger domestic supply chains to accelerate affordable EV manufacturing growth

items.title: Domestic CV sales to see moderate growth of 4-6% in FY27: ICRA
items.summary: The report noted that continued infrastructure spending, improved freight movement and replacement demand are expected to support volumes, although rising fuel costs and financing challenges remain key headwinds

items.title: ITC's packaged foods business crosses $2 billion in revenue
items.summary: ITC's packaged food business soared past $2 billion in FY26, with overall FMCG consumer spending reaching Rs 37,000 crore. This growth was fueled by premium product launches and a favorable tax environment. While the food segment remains the largest FMCG contributor, other businesses saw modest gains. The company faces challenges from increased cigarette taxation but remains optimistic about its market position.

items.title: ITC charts growth through FMCG, digital farming and fresh foods
items.summary: ITC Ltd is aggressively pursuing a medium-term growth strategy, aiming to become India's top FMCG player by bolstering core brands and exploring new health and convenience offerings. The company is also scaling its digital agriculture platform, ITCMAARS, to significantly boost farmer incomes and yields. Further expansion is planned for its fresh food business and sustainable packaging solutions, with a commitment to net-zero operations by 2050.

items.title: Responsible, inclusive AI adoption will accelerate India’s journey towards a developed nation, says PM Modi
items.summary: PM Modi said AI, when combined with human judgement and ethical practices, can improve efficiency, strengthen compliance and drive innovation across sectors

items.title: Indian Railways turns to automotive testing agency ARAI for First Full-Scale LHB coach crash test
items.summary: On June 24, Indian Railways physically crash-tested an LHB coach for the first time, deliberately crashing two passenger coaches into a fixed barrier at 44 kmph at its dedicated crash test facility in Lucknow

items.title: 1100% Dividend Payout: Swaraj Engines fixes record date for Rs 110 final dividend; stock in focus
items.summary: Shareholders looking to receive the proposed Rs 110 per share dividend must own the stock before the record date, with the payout awaiting approval at the company's 40th AGM.

items.title: Beyond ports and power, Adani is now selling organic fertilizers to farmers
items.summary: Waste-to-energy venture gives rise to a new farm-input-business as sales of ‘Harit Amrit’ brand of fertilisers expands across Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh & Gujarat

items.summary: The contract renewal reflects Indian Railways’ continued confidence in Alstom’s services and supports freight modernisation through electric locomotives

items.title: Why agricultural education must align with technology and sustainability
items.summary: Modernising agricultural education is an economic, environmental, and national imperative that will shape the resilience and sustainability of our food systems for decades to come

items.title: JK Cement convenes 32nd AGM; seeks approval for dividend & director appointments
items.summary: The board of directors has finalized July 10, 2026, as the record date to determine shareholder eligibility for the dividend pay-out, which will be distributed within 30 days of the conclusion of the AGM if approved

items.title: Department of Atomic Energy sets up world's first hydrogen production facility at IGCAR
items.summary: The facility was inaugurated by Ajit Kumar Mohanty, Secretary, Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) and chairman, Atomic Energy Commission (AEC), in the presence of Sreekumar G Pillai, Director, Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research (IGCAR).

items.title: South Indian Bank eyes inflows of up to $1 billion via fresh FCNR (B) deposits
items.summary: The sharp rise in interest rates on FCNR (B) deposits follows RBI’s measures to bolster Dollar inflows, including by bearing the full hedging cost for fresh 3–5-year FCNR (B) deposits raised up to September-end 2026

items.title: ITC’s non-cigarette FMCG business records ₹37,000 crore consumer spend in FY26
items.summary: ITC said its portfolio of over 30 brands reaches nearly 280 million households across India, supported by innovation and category expansion

items.title: SEBI rejects Anil Ambani settlement request over Reliance Infrastructure
items.summary: Anil Ambani, the younger brother ‌of billionaire Mukesh Ambani, has faced growing scrutiny from regulators and enforcement agencies over the past 18 months.

items.title: DGCA Inspects Boeing facility in US to review Air India B787 fuel control switch
items.summary: Inspection follows February 2 grounding of Air India 787-8 VT-ANX over fuel control switch issue

items.title: No smooth landing: ICRA sees Indian airlines' losses widening up to Rs 38,000 crore in FY27
items.summary: India's aviation sector faces a grim outlook, with ICRA projecting net losses to skyrocket to ₹36,000-38,000 crore in FY27. This sharp revision stems from a weaker rupee, soaring fuel costs, higher aircraft lease rentals, and subdued passenger demand, exacerbated by the West Asian conflict. He stressed the PM Gati Shakti Master Plan for timely completion and discussed TB Mukt Bharat Abhiyan, leveraging digital tech. The PM also addressed concerns over cybercrime and digital arrests, urging coordinated action and faster grievance redressal to protect citizens.

items.title: Sensex jumps over 700 points, Nifty rises above 24,200 as oil prices cool below $73 per barrel
items.summary: On Thursday, Indian stock markets experienced a remarkable surge, with both Sensex and Nifty seeing significant gains as oil prices fell below $73 per barrel. This decline in crude oil costs, along with a strengthening rupee, heightened investor enthusiasm. While the Realty and Auto sectors thrived, analysts express concern over potential adverse effects on rural-focused industries due to a deficient monsoon, although they endorse premium consumption and export-oriented sectors.

items.title: Interglobe Aviation among 5 F&O stocks with a sharp rise in futures open interest
items.summary: Five NSE F&O stocks, including Oracle Financial Services, IndiGo, IRFC, Swiggy and Avenue Supermarts, saw sharp open interest build-up on June 24, signaling heightened trader participation and fresh futures positions.

items.title: Bitcoin breaks below $60,000 again as biggest crypto buyers balk
items.summary: The cryptocurrency fell through a closely watched support level on Wednesday as investors grappled with growing concerns about Michael Saylor’s financing machine at Strategy Inc

items.title: JSW Ventures eyes  ₹400-500 crore third fund, plans larger startup bets
items.summary: The early-stage investor is looking to back 14-16 companies while increasing cheque sizes and expanding its focus on fintech, AI and sustainability.

items.title: India dodged the ‘Greenspan put’ but that era’s lessons for monetary policy must never be lost
items.summary: Recall the Great Recession that America suffered? Central bank heads globally must not forget how an asset bubble got inflated under the once celebrated US Federal Reserve chair’s watch. They should trust markets, but verify their role.

items.title: Mint Quick Edit | China's new ‘ethnic unity’ law suggests an attempt at global dominion
items.summary: The Chinese regime’s legislative move to keep people beyond China’s borders within the clutches of its notion of ethnic unity should firmly be rejected. This is nationalism gone wrong.

items.title: El Niño is set to further disrupt India's $300 billion farm supply chain
items.summary: India's crucial monsoon rains are delayed, disrupting the nation's $300 billion farm economy just as planting season begins. This agricultural setback, exacerbated by a developing El Niño, threatens staple crops like rice, cotton, and soybeans, impacting prices and potentially fueling inflation. While India's food system shows resilience, worsening climate conditions pose a growing challenge to this vital agricultural lifeline.

items.title: Dollar deposits look greener than domestic on RBI's hedge move
items.summary: RBI's latest measures to attract dollar deposits will likely give overseas Indians a better deal than local savers. The move has also split the lenders—those with a higher overseas access are chasing foreign currency deposits, while smaller lenders rely on local deposit rate hikes to attract funds.

items.title: For better outcomes, Centre nudges states on focused capex for  ₹2 tn loan scheme
items.summary: The DoE has asked states and UTs to limit their proposals under its SASCI scheme for capex to their choice of maximum five priority sectors in FY27. The move marks a shift from simply encouraging higher capex to ensuring more concentrated investments that deliver measurable development outcomes.

items.title: Commerce Minister Goyal to travel to U.K. to review preparedness for rollout of trade deal
items.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) and its companion Double Contribution Convention (DCC) will be implemented on July 15.

items.title: RBI says no to special exposure relaxations for state NBFCs
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has firmly rejected requests for special exemptions from concentration norms for government-owned NBFCs, emphasizing a principles-based regulatory approach. The central bank also denied calls to raise single counterparty limits for public sector enterprises. While allowing grandfathering of residual breaches and specific conditions for temporary limit breaches, the RBI has tightened rules to ensure regulatory clarity and stability.

items.title: RBI tightens rules on recovery of excess pension payments
items.summary: Government pensioners will now have stronger protection against unexpected deductions from their accounts. The Reserve Bank of India has mandated that banks must inform pensioners and obtain their consent before recovering any excess pension payments. This new directive ensures that individuals are aware and agree to any adjustments, preventing abrupt debits and requiring a written undertaking from pensioners for future recoveries.

items.title: Razor’s edge: On the progress of the southwest monsoon
items.summary: High heat and low rainfall is threatening agricultural produce

items.title: RBI in wait-and-watch mode, no certainty on rate hike: Governor Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated that an interest rate hike is premature, as inflation hasn't broadly impacted consumer prices. The central bank remains in a 'wait-and-watch' mode, closely monitoring global uncertainties and the monsoon's progress. While acknowledging improved outlooks, risks persist, but India is well-prepared for challenges. The RBI has revised inflation forecasts upwards and growth forecasts downwards for the fiscal year.

items.title: Gautam Adani’s lawyers urge U.S. Judge to dismiss criminal case
items.summary: Adani’s lawyer, Robert Giuffra, wrote ‌that the case should be dismissed because ​it was beyond the reach of U.S. law and because the prosecutors would ⁠not be able to prove the alleged bribery in India

items.title: S&P cuts India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.6%, sees inflation at 5.1%
items.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow to 6.6% in 2026-27 due to energy pressures, a weaker monsoon, and global slowdown, S&P Global Ratings reported. Retail inflation is expected to climb to 5.1% this fiscal year. Despite near-term challenges, GDP growth is anticipated to rebound to 7.2% in 2027-28, with oil prices expected to stabilize.

items.title: Goyal heads to UK ahead of India-UK trade pact rollout
items.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal's UK visit from June 25-27 precedes the July 15 implementation of the India-UK CETA and Double Contribution Convention. Discussions with UK's Business and Trade Secretary Peter Kyle will focus on regulatory alignment, customs coordination, and operationalizing tariff liberalisation for Indian exports. The talks also aim to boost professional mobility and achieve a bilateral trade target of $120 billion by 2030.

items.title: India, US inch closer to interim trade pact after fresh talks
items.summary: India and the US are nearing an interim trade deal, with both nations reporting significant progress in recent negotiations. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer concluded two days of talks, aiming to finalize the first phase of a bilateral trade agreement. Discussions focused on market access, digital trade, and non-tariff barriers, with both sides committed to a balanced pact delivering tangible benefits.

items.title: Exporters urge govt not to impose curbs on onions
items.summary: Indian onion exporters are urging the government against imposing export curbs, citing a surge in wholesale prices at Lasalgaon market. Delayed sowing and adverse weather have impacted production, pushing prices up. Exporters warn that restrictions could jeopardize markets painstakingly regained after previous bans, potentially shifting buyers to competing nations and damaging India's trade reputation.

items.title: Goyal meets exporters to discuss ways to boost shipments, leverage FTAs
items.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal met with exporters to strategize on boosting outbound shipments and maximizing opportunities through Free Trade Agreements. Discussions focused on strengthening MSME support, fostering innovation, improving export facilitation, and expanding India's global value chain role. The meeting reviewed strategies to enhance the export ecosystem, diversify markets, and boost trade competitiveness, with councils presenting their progress on key performance indicators.

Traders anticipate logistical hurdles and will assess goods stored for years before seeking government aid for potential losses.
